questionText: Which of the following removals accounting subcategories are relevant to your organization in the reporting year?
questionKo: 다음 중 removals accounting subcategories are relevant to 귀사 in the 보고연도에
sectorTags: Financial services, Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp:
The global scientific consensus makes it clear that the world needs to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions and remove carbon dioxide (CO2) from the atmosphere to avoid a 1.5°C (or even 2°C) rise in global average temperature compared to pre-industrial levels. Both land sector mitigation approaches along with new CO2 removal technologies will play an important role in reducing, halting, and ultimately reversing, the accumulation of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere.
The Greenhouse Gas Protocol Land Sector and Removals Standard (LSRS) establishes requirements and recommendations to account for, report and track GHG emissions, CO2 removals and other relevant metrics that reflect anthropogenic activities in the land sector, as well as other CO2 removals technologies.

rows.title: Captured biogenic CO2 with geologic storage
rows.titleKo: Captured biogenic CO2 with geologic storage

rows.title: Technological CO2 removals with geologic storage
rows.titleKo: Technological CO2 removals with geologic storage

rows.title: TCDR-based product CO2 emissions (as gross CO2 fluxes)
rows.titleKo: TCDR-based product CO2 emissions (as gross CO2 fluxes)

rows.title: Gross CO2 emissions from geologic storage
rows.titleKo: Gross CO2 emissions from geologic storage

rows.title: Reversals of CO2 removals with geologic storage
rows.titleKo: Reversals of CO2 removals with geologic storage

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes, in our direct operations", "Yes, in our upstream/downstream value chain" or "Yes, in both our direct operations and our upstream/downstream value chain" in response to 7.13 column "Technological CO2 removals or captured CO2 with geologic storage in the reporting year", or if you select "Yes" in response to 7.13 column "Technological CO2 removals or captured CO2 with geologic storage were accounted for and reported in previous years"
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Yes, in our 직접 운영사업장", "Yes, in our upstream/다운스트림 가치사슬" 또는 "Yes, in both our 직접 운영사업장 및 our upstream/다운스트림 가치사슬" in response to 7.13 열 "Technological CO2 removals 또는 captured CO2 with geologic storage in the 보고연도", 또는 만약 you select "Yes" in response to 7.13 열 "Technological CO2 removals 또는 captured CO2 with geologic storage were accounted for 및 보고 in previous years"

guidance.text:
General 
This question asks which of the GHG Protocol Land Sector and Removals Standard (LSRS) physical GHG inventory accounting subcategories and additional accounting subcategories for technological removal and captured CO2 with geologic storage value chains are relevant to your organization.
The evaluation status includes two components: whether an accounting subcategory is relevant to your business and whether you have calculated the data in that subcategory.
Relevance may be affected by your significance threshold. The GHG Protocol makes no specific recommendations as to what constitutes a "significant" exclusion threshold. If you deem a subcategory not relevant on the basis of your significance threshold, provide the details of the threshold applied in the "Please explain" column.
Note: if you have calculated emissions in line with the LSRS for the first time and this has resulted in changes to the scope 1, scope 2 and/or cope 3 emissions figures reported in 7.6, 7.7 and/or 7.8, report this as a change in methodology in 7.1.2 and report details of the change in emissions as a result of the change in methodology in 7.10.1 and 7.11.1 (if applicable).
For more details on each of the subcategories, see the "Explanation of Terms" and refer to the GHG Protocol Land Sector and Removals Standard and Guidance.
TCDR-based product CO2 emissions (as gross CO2 fluxes) (row 3) 
According to "Requirement 18: TCDR-based product emissions accounting" of the LSRS, CO2 emissions from technological carbon dioxide removal (TCDR)-based products should be reported as gross CO2 fluxes (separately from "Fossil fuel and industrial emissions") in the scope corresponding to the point of oxidation (i.e., when they are released to the atmosphere) if:
you have information on the origin of the CO2 or carbon in the product that demonstrates the CO2 is technologically removed CO2; and
you account for and report all life cycle GHG emissions associated with the TCDR-based product in scope 3.
Select one of the "Relevant…" options if you meet these criteria for any TCDR-based products.
If you do not meet these criteria for any TCDR-based products, select "Not relevant, explanation provided" and report CO2 emissions from TCDR-based products as "Fossil fuel and industrial emissions".
Note that "Fossil fuel and industrial emissions" are currently part of the emissions figures reported in questions 7.6, 7.7 and 7.8.
Reversals of CO2 removals with geologic storage (row 5) 
Reversals are emissions from carbon pools which store carbon associated with previously reported removals in cases where the carbon pool is no longer within the company's operations or value chain.
In cases where the carbon pool remains within the organization's operations or value chain, any associated emissions should be reported as "Fossil fuel and industrial emissions" (in 7.6 and/or 7.8).
If you have never previously reported CO2 removals with geologic storage, select "Not relevant, explanation provided" in this row, and use the "Please explain" column to explain that this is the reason why reversals are not relevant.
Note that in 2026, organizations are not expected to report reversals of CO2 removals with geologic storage since 2026 is the first year they are able to report CO2 removals with geologic storage in line with the LSRS.
Please explain (column 4) 
For all accounting subcategories identified as "Not relevant, explanation provided" in columns "Scope 1", "Scope 2", and/or "Scope 3", explain how you have reached the conclusion that the subcategory is not relevant and include details of any significance thresholds applied.
If relevant, use this column to explain your approach to reporting TCDR-based product emissions, including whether these emissions are currently included in 7.6, 7.7 and/or 7.8. See LSRS "Requirement 18: TCDR-based product emissions accounting" for further details.
guidance.textKo: 일반 This 문항 asks which 의 GHG Protocol Land Sector 및 Removals Standard (LSRS) physical GHG inventory accounting subcategories 및 additional accounting subcategories for technological removal 및 captured CO2 with geologic storage 가치사슬s are 관련 to 귀사. The evaluation status includes two components: whether an accounting subcategory is 관련 to your business 및 whether you have calculated the data in that subcategory. Relevance may be affected by your significance threshold. The GHG Protocol makes no specific recommendations as to what constitutes a "significant" exclusion threshold. 만약 you deem a subcategory not 관련 on the basis of your significance threshold, provide the details 의 threshold applied in the "Please explain" 열. Note: 만약 you have calculated emissions in line with the LSRS 에 대한 first time 및 this has resulted in changes to the scope 1, scope 2 및/또는 cope 3 emissions figures 보고 in 7.6, 7.7 및/또는 7.8, report this as a change in 방법론 in 7.1.2 및 report details 의 change in emissions as a result 의 change in 방법론 in 7.10.1 및 7.11.1 (만약 해당되는 경우). For more details on each 의 subcategories, see the "설명 of Terms" 및 refer to the GHG Protocol Land Sector 및 Removals Standard 및 Guidance. TCDR-based product CO2 emissions (as gross CO2 fluxes) (행 3) According to "Requirement 18: TCDR-based product 배출량 회계" 의 LSRS, CO2 emissions from technological carbon dioxide removal (TCDR)-based products 해야 합니다 be 보고 as gross CO2 fluxes (separately from "Fossil fuel 및 industrial emissions") in the scope corresponding to the 점 of oxidation (i.e., 경우 they are released to the atmosphere) 만약: you have information on the origin 의 CO2 또는 carbon in the product that demonstrates the CO2 is technologically 삭제 CO2; 및 you account for 및 report all life cycle 온실가스 배출량 associated with the TCDR-based product in scope 3. Select one 의 "관련…" options 만약 you meet these 기준 for 하나 이상 TCDR-based products. 만약 you do not meet these 기준 for 하나 이상 TCDR-based products, select "Not 관련, 설명 입력" 및 report CO2 emissions from TCDR-based products as "Fossil fuel 및 industrial emissions". Note that "Fossil fuel 및 industrial emissions" are currently part 의 emissions figures 보고 in 문항 7.6, 7.7 및 7.8. Reversals of CO2 removals with geologic storage (행 5) Reversals are emissions from carbon pools which store carbon associated with previously 보고 removals in cases 여기서 the carbon pool is no longer within the company's operations 또는 가치사슬. In cases 여기서 the carbon pool remains within the 조직의 operations 또는 가치사슬, 하나 이상 associated emissions 해야 합니다 be 보고 as "Fossil fuel 및 industrial emissions" (in 7.6 및/또는 7.8). 만약 you have never previously 보고 CO2 removals with geologic storage, select "Not 관련, 설명 입력" in this 행, 및 use the "Please explain" 열 to explain that this is the reason why reversals are not 관련. Note that in 2026, organizations are not expected to report reversals of CO2 removals with geologic storage since 2026 is the first year they are able to report CO2 removals with geologic storage in line with the LSRS. Please explain (열 4) For all accounting subcategories identified as "Not 관련, 설명 입력" in 열 "Scope 1", "Scope 2", 및/또는 "Scope 3", explain how you have reached the conclusion that the subcategory is not 관련 및 include details of 하나 이상 significance thresholds applied. 만약 관련, use this 열 to explain your approach to reporting TCDR-based product emissions, 포함 whether these emissions are currently included in 7.6, 7.7 및/또는 7.8. See LSRS "Requirement 18: TCDR-based product 배출량 회계" for further details.

guidance.text:
Captured biogenic CO2 with geologic storage: net CO2 removals resulting from annual net increases to carbon stored in geologic carbon pools from carbon derived from biological sinks ( GHG Protocol ).
Gross CO2 emissions from geologic storage: gross CO2 emissions from fugitive CO2 emissions or other CO2 losses to the atmosphere from a geologic reservoir containing captured and stored CO2 ( GHG Protocol ).
Reversals: an emission from a carbon pool that stores carbon associated with a removal that was previously reported by the reporting organization, and that it is no longer within the inventory boundary ( GHG Protocol ).
TCDR-based product CO2 emissions (as gross CO2 fluxes): gross CO2 emissions from the combustion, degradation or other losses from technological carbon dioxide removal-based product carbon pools to the atmosphere ( GHG Protocol ).
Technological CO2 removals with geologic storage: net CO2 removals resulting from annual net increases to carbon stored in geologic carbon pools from carbon derived from technological sinks ( GHG Protocol ).
guidance.textKo: Captured biogenic CO2 with geologic storage: net CO2 removals resulting from annual net increases to carbon stored in geologic carbon pools from carbon derived from biological sinks (GHG Protocol). Gross CO2 emissions from geologic storage: gross CO2 emissions from fugitive CO2 emissions 또는 other CO2 losses to the atmosphere from a geologic reservoir containing captured 및 stored CO2 (GHG Protocol). Reversals: an emission from a carbon pool that stores carbon associated with a removal that was previously 보고 by the reporting organization, 및 that it is no longer within the inventory boundary (GHG Protocol). TCDR-based product CO2 emissions (as gross CO2 fluxes): gross CO2 emissions from the combustion, degradation 또는 other losses from technological carbon dioxide removal-based product carbon pools to the atmosphere (GHG Protocol). Technological CO2 removals with geologic storage: net CO2 removals resulting from annual net increases to carbon stored in geologic carbon pools from carbon derived from technological sinks (GHG Protocol).

change.question_ko: [국문 번역 검토 필요] Which of the following removals accounting subcategories are relevant to your organization in the reporting year?
change.scoring_change_summary: CC All sectors Disclosure, Awareness & Management • Question scored for completion of the table. Proportionally at Disclosure level and for a complete table at Awareness level. • Management level is not scored. Scoring updated to account for change in question.

2026 question text applied
2026 response type/columns applied
2026 D/A/M/L point allocation linked
2026 scoring criteria linked

sectorTagsKo: 금융 서비스, 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 OEM - EPM, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 농산물 원자재, 운송 OEM, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 시멘트, Food, beverage & tobacco, 제지 및 임업, 부동산, 건설
simpleHelpKo: The global scientific consensus makes it clear that the world needs to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions 및 remove carbon dioxide (CO2) from the atmosphere to avoid a 1.5°C (or even 2°C) rise in global average temperature compared to pre-industrial levels. Both land sector mitigation approaches along with 신규 CO2 removal technologies will play an important role in reducing, halting, 및 ultimately reversing, the accumulation of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere. The Greenhouse Gas Protocol Land Sector 및 Removals Standard (LSRS) establishes requirements 및 recommendations to account for, report 및 track 온실가스 배출량, CO2 removals 및 other 관련 metrics that reflect anthropogenic activities in the land sector, as well as other CO2 removals technologies.
